evidence_label: compact PubMed quote-gate cohesion refresh, not treatment advice
source: NCBI PubMed ESearch and ESummary plus direct PubMed page checks
query_focus: 2026 thalassemia HbF/HBG, assay, erythroid, and benchmark context

records.role: HBG promoter-editing benchmark; supports rejecting HbF-only novelty claims
records.title: CRISPR-Cas12a Gene Editing of HBG1 and HBG2 Promoters to Treat beta-Thalassemia

records.role: CS-101 base-editing benchmark; keeps HbF restoration from being treated as novel by itself
records.title: Clinical application of base editing for treating beta-thalassaemia

records.role: HBG promoter methylation biology; strengthens HbF mechanism context but not quote sufficiency
records.title: DNA methylation matters: methylation of the gamma-globin (HBG) gene promoters is required for postnatal silencing of HbF

records.role: non-editing HbF pharmacology signal; high-caution assay benchmark only
records.title: Discovery of a Novel DNMT1 Inhibitor with Improved Efficacy in Treating beta-Thalassemia

records.role: gamma-globin regulation context; reinforces mechanism diversity behind HbF signals
records.title: p27Kip1 regulates gamma-globin production

records.role: newer HbF-modifier genetics context; background only for quote acceptance
records.title: Genetic polymorphism of SNPs rs9399137 and rs4895441in HBS1L-MYB and SNP rs766432 in BCL11A among beta-thalassemia Egyptian patients

decision_use: The June 12 check found no reason to loosen the June 10 quote gate. HbF or HBG remains necessary but insufficient without maturation, viability, alpha-globin or autophagy, hemolysis or membrane-safety, raw-data, cost, timeline, ethics, biosafety, and material constraints.
